intro-1-t0.plaintext: A listing block fetches content from the server (e.g. latest news) and renders each result as a separate block. expandListingBlocks(layout, options) walks a layout, fetches results for each listing-type block, and returns { items, paging } where items is an array of block objects with @uid and @type. You tell it which block types need fetching via a fetchItems map — keys are block types, values are fetcher functions. This means you can have different kinds of listings (Plone queries, RSS feeds, etc.) each with their own fetcher:

example-text-t0.plaintext: A grid can have a mix of listing and static blocks sharing a single paging. The staticBlocks helper wraps non-listing blocks so they participate in the shared page window. The listings use Suspense so they load client-side:

children.text: A grid can have a mix of listing and static blocks sharing a single paging. The
helper wraps non-listing blocks so they participate in the shared page window. The listings use Suspense so they load client-side:

tabs.label: JSX

options-text.plaintext: Options for expandListingBlocks: blocks, fetchItems (type→fetcher map), paging, seen, itemTypeField, defaultItemType.

children.text: — Map of blockId to block data
— Required. Map of { blockType: async (block, { start, size }) => { items, total } }. Keys declare which block types to expand; values are fetcher functions. Use ploneFetchItems() for Plone backends.
— Paging input { start, size } (not mutated). Computed values are returned in the response.
— Number of items already seen by prior calls (default: 0). Chain paging.seen from one call to the next for grids.
— Field on the listing block that holds the item type (default: 'itemType')
— Fallback type when field is not set (default: 'summary')

plonefetch-text.plaintext: ploneFetchItems creates a fetcher function for Plone backends, suitable as a value in the fetchItems map.

children.text: — creates a fetcher function for Plone backends, suitable as a value in the fetchItems map. Normalizes results by packaging image_field + image_scales into a self-contained image object { @id, image_field, image_scales }.
For non-Plone backends (RSS feeds, external APIs, etc.), write your own fetcher: async (block, { start, size }) => { items, total }.

fieldmapping-text-t0.plaintext: fieldMapping on a listing block controls which fields appear on expanded items — only mapped fields are included. Default: { @id → href, title → title, description → description, image → image }. Values can be a string (rename) or { field, type } for conversions:

children.text: on a listing block controls which fields appear on expanded items — only mapped fields are included. Default: { @id → href, title → title, description → description, image → image }. Values can be a string (rename) or { field, type } for conversions:

tabs.label: JSON

itemtype-text-t0.plaintext: Use itemType (or variation) on the listing block to control what @type expanded items get. Combined with inheritSchemaFrom, the listing's sidebar shows fields from the selected item type:

, the listing's sidebar shows fields from the selected item type:

tabs.label: JavaScript

paging-text.plaintext: Both expandListingBlocks and staticBlocks return { items, paging }. You pass { start, size } as input (not mutated) and get back computed paging values:

children.text: return { items, paging }. You pass { start, size } as input (not mutated) and get back computed paging values:
(number) — Total item count across all blocks
(number | null) — Previous page index, or null on first page
(number | null) — Next page index, or null on last page
(array) — Window of ~5 page objects: { start, page } where page is 1-based
(number) — Running item count — pass to the next call's seen option for position tracking in grids

notes-text.plaintext: Expanded listing items share the listing block's @uid. Selecting any expanded item selects the parent listing block.

children.text: Expanded listing items share the listing block's @uid. Selecting any expanded item selects the parent listing block.

pathtransform-text-t0.plaintext: If your frontend embeds state in the URL path (like pagination), you need to tell hydra.js how to transform the frontend path to the API/admin path. Otherwise, the admin will try to navigate to URLs that don't exist in the CMS.

children.text: If your frontend embeds state in the URL path (like pagination), you need to tell hydra.js how to transform the frontend path to the API/admin path. Otherwise, the admin will try to navigate to URLs that don't exist in the CMS.

tabs.label: JavaScript

pathtransform-text-t2.plaintext: The pathToApiPath function is called whenever hydra.js sends a PATH_CHANGE message to the admin, allowing your frontend to strip or transform URL segments that are frontend-specific (like pagination, filters, or other client-side state).

children.text: function is called whenever hydra.js sends a PATH_CHANGE message to the admin, allowing your frontend to strip or transform URL segments that are frontend-specific (like pagination, filters, or other client-side state).
